api_types.name: WorkflowArtifactGCTask
api_types.doc: WorkflowArtifactGCTask specifies the Artifacts that need to be deleted as well as the status of deletion +genclient +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: ArtifactGCSpec
api_types.doc: ArtifactGCSpec specifies the Artifacts that need to be deleted

fields.name: ArtifactsByNode
fields.doc: ArtifactsByNode maps Node name to information pertaining to Artifacts on that Node

api_types.name: ArtifactNodeSpec
api_types.doc: ArtifactNodeSpec specifies the Artifacts that need to be deleted for a given Node

fields.name: ArchiveLocation
fields.doc: ArchiveLocation is the template-level Artifact location specification

fields.name: Artifacts
fields.doc: Artifacts maps artifact name to Artifact description

api_types.name: ArtifactGCStatus
api_types.doc: ArtifactGCStatus describes the result of the deletion

fields.name: ArtifactResultsByNode
fields.doc: ArtifactResultsByNode maps Node name to result

api_types.name: ArtifactResultNodeStatus
api_types.doc: ArtifactResultNodeStatus describes the result of the deletion on a given node

fields.name: ArtifactResults
fields.doc: ArtifactResults maps Artifact name to result of the deletion

api_types.name: ArtifactResult
api_types.doc: ArtifactResult describes the result of attempting to delete a given Artifact

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the name of the Artifact

fields.name: Success
fields.doc: Success describes whether the deletion succeeded

fields.name: Error
fields.doc: Error is an optional error message which should be set if Success==false

api_types.doc: WorkflowArtifactGCTaskList is list of WorkflowArtifactGCTask resources +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: GCSArtifactRepository
api_types.doc: GCSArtifactRepository defines the controller configuration for a GCS artifact repository

fields.name: KeyFormat
fields.doc: KeyFormat defines the format of how to store keys and can reference workflow variables.

api_types.name: ArtifactoryArtifactRepository
api_types.doc: ArtifactoryArtifactRepository defines the controller configuration for an artifactory artifact repository

fields.name: RepoURL
fields.doc: RepoURL is the url for artifactory repo.

fields.name: KeyFormat
fields.doc: KeyFormat defines the format of how to store keys and can reference workflow variables.

api_types.name: AzureArtifactRepository
api_types.doc: AzureArtifactRepository defines the controller configuration for an Azure Blob Storage artifact repository

fields.name: BlobNameFormat
fields.doc: BlobNameFormat is defines the format of how to store blob names. Can reference workflow variables

api_types.name: ArtifactRepository
api_types.doc: ArtifactRepository represents an artifact repository in which a controller will store its artifacts

fields.name: ArchiveLogs
fields.doc: ArchiveLogs enables log archiving

fields.name: S3
fields.doc: S3 stores artifact in a S3-compliant object store

fields.name: Artifactory
fields.doc: Artifactory stores artifacts to JFrog Artifactory

fields.name: HDFS
fields.doc: HDFS stores artifacts in HDFS

fields.name: OSS
fields.doc: OSS stores artifact in a OSS-compliant object store

fields.name: GCS
fields.doc: GCS stores artifact in a GCS object store

fields.name: Azure
fields.doc: Azure stores artifact in an Azure Storage account

api_types.name: HDFSArtifactRepository
api_types.doc: HDFSArtifactRepository defines the controller configuration for an HDFS artifact repository

fields.name: PathFormat
fields.doc: PathFormat is defines the format of path to store a file. Can reference workflow variables

fields.name: Force
fields.doc: Force copies a file forcibly even if it exists

api_types.name: S3ArtifactRepository
api_types.doc: S3ArtifactRepository defines the controller configuration for an S3 artifact repository

fields.name: KeyFormat
fields.doc: KeyFormat defines the format of how to store keys and can reference workflow variables.

fields.name: KeyPrefix
fields.doc: KeyPrefix is prefix used as part of the bucket key in which the controller will store artifacts. DEPRECATED. Use KeyFormat instead

api_types.name: OSSArtifactRepository
api_types.doc: OSSArtifactRepository defines the controller configuration for an OSS artifact repository

fields.name: KeyFormat
fields.doc: KeyFormat defines the format of how to store keys and can reference workflow variables.

api_types.name: ClusterWorkflowTemplate
api_types.doc: ClusterWorkflowTemplate is the definition of a workflow template resource in cluster scope +genclient +genclient:noStatus +genclient:nonNamespaced +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: ClusterWorkflowTemplateList
api_types.doc: ClusterWorkflowTemplateList is list of ClusterWorkflowTemplate resources +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

fields.name: RetryStrategy
fields.doc: RetryStrategy describes how to retry container nodes if the container set fails. Note that this works differently from the template-level `retryStrategy` as it is a process-level retry that does not create new Pods or containers.

api_types.name: ContainerSetRetryStrategy
api_types.doc: ContainerSetRetryStrategy provides controls on how to retry a container set

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration is the time between each retry, examples values are "300ms", "1s" or "5m". Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".

fields.name: Retries
fields.doc: Retries is the maximum number of retry attempts for each container. It does not include the first, original attempt; the maximum number of total attempts will be `retries + 1`.

api_types.name: CronWorkflow
api_types.doc: CronWorkflow is the definition of a scheduled workflow resource +genclient +genclient:noStatus +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: CronWorkflowList
api_types.doc: CronWorkflowList is list of CronWorkflow resources +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: CronWorkflowSpec
api_types.doc: CronWorkflowSpec is the specification of a CronWorkflow

fields.name: WorkflowSpec
fields.doc: WorkflowSpec is the spec of the workflow to be run

fields.name: Schedule
fields.doc: Schedule is a schedule to run the Workflow in Cron format. Deprecated, use Schedules

fields.name: ConcurrencyPolicy
fields.doc: ConcurrencyPolicy is the K8s-style concurrency policy that will be used

fields.name: Suspend
fields.doc: Suspend is a flag that will stop new CronWorkflows from running if set to true

fields.name: StartingDeadlineSeconds
fields.doc: StartingDeadlineSeconds is the K8s-style deadline that will limit the time a CronWorkflow will be run after its original scheduled time if it is missed.

fields.name: SuccessfulJobsHistoryLimit
fields.doc: SuccessfulJobsHistoryLimit is the number of successful jobs to be kept at a time

fields.name: FailedJobsHistoryLimit
fields.doc: FailedJobsHistoryLimit is the number of failed jobs to be kept at a time

fields.name: Timezone
fields.doc: Timezone is the timezone against which the cron schedule will be calculated, e.g. "Asia/Tokyo". Default is machine's local time.

fields.name: WorkflowMetadata
fields.doc: WorkflowMetadata contains some metadata of the workflow to be run

fields.name: StopStrategy
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: StopStrategy defines if the CronWorkflow should stop scheduling based on a condition

fields.name: Schedules
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: Schedules is a list of schedules to run the Workflow in Cron format

fields.name: When
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: When is an expression that determines if a run should be scheduled.

api_types.name: StopStrategy
api_types.doc: StopStrategy defines if the CronWorkflow should stop scheduling based on an expression. v3.6 and after

fields.name: Expression
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: Expression is an expression that stops scheduling workflows when true. Use the variables `cronworkflow`.`failed` or `cronworkflow`.`succeeded` to access the number of failed or successful child workflows.

api_types.name: CronWorkflowStatus
api_types.doc: CronWorkflowStatus is the status of a CronWorkflow

fields.name: Active
fields.doc: Active is a list of active workflows stemming from this CronWorkflow

fields.name: LastScheduledTime
fields.doc: LastScheduleTime is the last time the CronWorkflow was scheduled

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Conditions is a list of conditions the CronWorkflow may have

fields.name: Succeeded
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: Succeeded counts how many times child workflows succeeded

fields.name: Failed
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: Failed counts how many times child workflows failed

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: Phase is an enum of Active or Stopped. It changes to Stopped when stopStrategy.expression is true

fields.name: Expression
fields.doc: Expression defines an expr expression to apply

api_types.name: DataSource
api_types.doc: DataSource sources external data into a data template

fields.name: ArtifactPaths
fields.doc: ArtifactPaths is a data transformation that collects a list of artifact paths

api_types.name: ArtifactPaths
api_types.doc: ArtifactPaths expands a step from a collection of artifacts

fields.name: Artifact
fields.doc: Artifact is the artifact location from which to source the artifacts, it can be a directory

api_types.name: Data
api_types.doc: Data is a data template

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Source sources external data into a data template

fields.name: Transformation
fields.doc: Transformation applies a set of transformations

api_types.name: WorkflowEventBinding
api_types.doc: WorkflowEventBinding is the definition of an event resource +genclient +genclient:noStatus +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: WorkflowEventBindingList
api_types.doc: WorkflowEventBindingList is list of event resources +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

fields.name: Event
fields.doc: Event is the event to bind to

fields.name: Submit
fields.doc: Submit is the workflow template to submit

fields.name: Selector
fields.doc: Selector (https://github.com/expr-lang/expr) that we must must match the event. E.g. `payload.message == "test"`

fields.name: WorkflowTemplateRef
fields.doc: WorkflowTemplateRef the workflow template to submit

fields.name: ObjectMeta
fields.doc: Metadata optional means to customize select fields of the workflow metadata

fields.name: Arguments
fields.doc: Arguments extracted from the event and then set as arguments to the workflow created.

api_types.name: HTTPBodySource
api_types.doc: HTTPBodySource contains the source of the HTTP body.

fields.name: Method
fields.doc: Method is HTTP methods for HTTP Request

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: URL of the HTTP Request

fields.name: Headers
fields.doc: Headers are an optional list of headers to send with HTTP requests

fields.name: TimeoutSeconds
fields.doc: TimeoutSeconds is request timeout for HTTP Request. Default is 30 seconds

fields.name: SuccessCondition
fields.doc: SuccessCondition is an expression if evaluated to true is considered successful

fields.name: Body
fields.doc: Body is content of the HTTP Request

fields.name: BodyFrom
fields.doc: BodyFrom is  content of the HTTP Request as Bytes

fields.name: InsecureSkipVerify
fields.doc: InsecureSkipVerify is a bool when if set to true will skip TLS verification for the HTTP client

api_types.name: Plugin
api_types.doc: Plugin is an Object with exactly one key

api_types.name: WorkflowTaskResult
api_types.doc: WorkflowTaskResult is a used to communicate a result back to the controller. Unlike WorkflowTaskSet, it has more capacity. This is an internal type. Users should never create this resource directly, much like you would never create a ReplicaSet directly. +genclient +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: WorkflowTaskResultList
api_types.doc: +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: WorkflowTaskSet
api_types.doc: +genclient +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: WorkflowTaskSetList
api_types.doc: +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: WorkflowTemplate
api_types.doc: WorkflowTemplate is the definition of a workflow template resource +genclient +genclient:noStatus +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: WorkflowTemplateList
api_types.doc: WorkflowTemplateList is list of WorkflowTemplate resources +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: PodGC
api_types.doc: PodGC describes how to delete completed pods as they complete

fields.name: Strategy
fields.doc: Strategy is the strategy to use. One of "OnPodCompletion", "OnPodSuccess", "OnWorkflowCompletion", "OnWorkflowSuccess". If unset, does not delete Pods

fields.name: LabelSelector
fields.doc: LabelSelector is the label selector to check if the pods match the labels before being added to the pod GC queue.

fields.name: DeleteDelayDuration
fields.doc: DeleteDelayDuration specifies the duration before pods in the GC queue get deleted.

api_types.name: WorkflowLevelArtifactGC
api_types.doc: WorkflowLevelArtifactGC describes how to delete artifacts from completed Workflows - this spec is used on the Workflow level

fields.name: ArtifactGC
fields.doc: ArtifactGC is an embedded struct

fields.name: ForceFinalizerRemoval
fields.doc: ForceFinalizerRemoval: if set to true, the finalizer will be removed in the case that Artifact GC fails

fields.name: PodSpecPatch
fields.doc: PodSpecPatch holds strategic merge patch to apply against the artgc pod spec.

api_types.name: ArtifactGC
api_types.doc: ArtifactGC describes how to delete artifacts from completed Workflows - this is embedded into the WorkflowLevelArtifactGC, and also used for individual Artifacts to override that as needed

fields.name: Strategy
fields.doc: Strategy is the strategy to use.

fields.name: PodMetadata
fields.doc: PodMetadata is an optional field for specifying the Labels and Annotations that should be assigned to the Pod doing the deletion

fields.name: ServiceAccountName
fields.doc: ServiceAccountName is an optional field for specifying the Service Account that should be assigned to the Pod doing the deletion

api_types.name: VolumeClaimGC
api_types.doc: VolumeClaimGC describes how to delete volumes from completed Workflows

fields.name: Strategy
fields.doc: Strategy is the strategy to use. One of "OnWorkflowCompletion", "OnWorkflowSuccess". Defaults to "OnWorkflowSuccess"

api_types.name: ArchiveStrategy
api_types.doc: ArchiveStrategy describes how to archive files/directory when saving artifacts

api_types.name: TarStrategy
api_types.doc: TarStrategy will tar and gzip the file or directory when saving

fields.name: CompressionLevel
fields.doc: CompressionLevel specifies the gzip compression level to use for the artifact. Defaults to gzip.DefaultCompression.

api_types.name: ArtifactLocation
api_types.doc: ArtifactLocation describes a location for a single or multiple artifacts. It is used as single artifact in the context of inputs/outputs (e.g. outputs.artifacts.artname). It is also used to describe the location of multiple artifacts such as the archive location of a single workflow step, which the executor will use as a default location to store its files.

fields.name: ArchiveLogs
fields.doc: ArchiveLogs indicates if the container logs should be archived

fields.name: S3
fields.doc: S3 contains S3 artifact location details

fields.name: Git
fields.doc: Git contains git artifact location details

fields.name: HTTP
fields.doc: HTTP contains HTTP artifact location details

fields.name: Artifactory
fields.doc: Artifactory contains artifactory artifact location details

fields.name: HDFS
fields.doc: HDFS contains HDFS artifact location details

fields.name: Raw
fields.doc: Raw contains raw artifact location details

fields.name: OSS
fields.doc: OSS contains OSS artifact location details

fields.name: GCS
fields.doc: GCS contains GCS artifact location details

fields.name: Azure
fields.doc: Azure contains Azure Storage artifact location details

api_types.name: ArtifactRepositoryRef
api_types.doc: +protobuf.options.(gogoproto.goproto_stringer)=false

fields.name: ConfigMap
fields.doc: The name of the config map. Defaults to "artifact-repositories".

fields.name: Key
fields.doc: The config map key. Defaults to the value of the "workflows.argoproj.io/default-artifact-repository" annotation.

api_types.name: ArtifactRepositoryRefStatus
api_types.doc: +protobuf.options.(gogoproto.goproto_stringer)=false

fields.name: Namespace
fields.doc: The namespace of the config map. Defaults to the workflow's namespace, or the controller's namespace (if found).

fields.name: Default
fields.doc: If this ref represents the default artifact repository, rather than a config map.

fields.name: ArtifactRepository
fields.doc: The repository the workflow will use. This maybe empty before v3.1.

api_types.name: ArtGCStatus
api_types.doc: ArtGCStatus maintains state related to ArtifactGC

fields.name: StrategiesProcessed
fields.doc: have Pods been started to perform this strategy? (enables us not to re-process what we've already done)

fields.name: PodsRecouped
fields.doc: have completed Pods been processed? (mapped by Pod name) used to prevent re-processing the Status of a Pod more than once

fields.name: NotSpecified
fields.doc: if this is true, we already checked to see if we need to do it and we don't

api_types.name: Outputs
api_types.doc: Outputs hold parameters, artifacts, and results from a step

fields.name: Parameters
fields.doc: Parameters holds the list of output parameters produced by a step +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: Artifacts
fields.doc: Artifacts holds the list of output artifacts produced by a step +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: Result
fields.doc: Result holds the result (stdout) of a script template

fields.name: ExitCode
fields.doc: ExitCode holds the exit code of a script template

api_types.name: WorkflowStep
api_types.doc: WorkflowStep is a reference to a template to execute in a series of step

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name of the step

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Template is the name of the template to execute as the step

fields.name: Inline
fields.doc: Inline is the template. Template must be empty if this is declared (and vice-versa).

fields.name: Arguments
fields.doc: Arguments hold arguments to the template

fields.name: TemplateRef
fields.doc: TemplateRef is the reference to the template resource to execute as the step.

fields.name: WithItems
fields.doc: WithItems expands a step into multiple parallel steps from the items in the list

fields.name: WithParam
fields.doc: WithParam expands a step into multiple parallel steps from the value in the parameter, which is expected to be a JSON list.

fields.name: WithSequence
fields.doc: WithSequence expands a step into a numeric sequence

fields.name: When
fields.doc: When is an expression in which the step should conditionally execute

fields.name: ContinueOn
fields.doc: ContinueOn makes argo to proceed with the following step even if this step fails. Errors and Failed states can be specified

fields.name: OnExit
fields.doc: OnExit is a template reference which is invoked at the end of the template, irrespective of the success, failure, or error of the primary template. DEPRECATED: Use Hooks[exit].Template instead.

fields.name: Hooks
fields.doc: Hooks holds the lifecycle hook which is invoked at lifecycle of step, irrespective of the success, failure, or error status of the primary step

api_types.name: Workflow
api_types.doc: Workflow is the definition of a workflow resource +genclient +genclient:noStatus +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object
markers: +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="Message",type="string",JSONPath=".status.message",description="Human readable message indicating details about why the workflow is in this condition."

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Template is the name of the template to execute by the hook

fields.name: Arguments
fields.doc: Arguments hold arguments to the template

fields.name: TemplateRef
fields.doc: TemplateRef is the reference to the template resource to execute by the hook

fields.name: Expression
fields.doc: Expression is a condition expression for when a node will be retried. If it evaluates to false, the node will not be retried and the retry strategy will be ignored

api_types.name: Sequence
api_types.doc: Sequence expands a workflow step into numeric range

fields.name: Count
fields.doc: Count is number of elements in the sequence (default: 0). Not to be used with end

fields.name: Start
fields.doc: Number at which to start the sequence (default: 0)

fields.name: End
fields.doc: Number at which to end the sequence (default: 0). Not to be used with Count

fields.name: Format
fields.doc: Format is a printf format string to format the value in the sequence

api_types.name: TemplateRef
api_types.doc: TemplateRef is a reference of template resource.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the resource name of the template.

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Template is the name of referred template in the resource.

fields.name: ClusterScope
fields.doc: ClusterScope indicates the referred template is cluster scoped (i.e. a ClusterWorkflowTemplate).

api_types.name: Synchronization
api_types.doc: Synchronization holds synchronization lock configuration

fields.name: Semaphore
fields.doc: Semaphore holds the Semaphore configuration - deprecated, use semaphores instead

fields.name: Mutex
fields.doc: Mutex holds the Mutex lock details - deprecated, use mutexes instead

fields.name: Semaphores
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: Semaphores holds the list of Semaphores configuration

fields.name: Mutexes
fields.doc: v3.6 and after: Mutexes holds the list of Mutex lock details

api_types.name: SemaphoreRef
api_types.doc: SemaphoreRef is a reference of Semaphore

fields.name: ConfigMapKeyRef
fields.doc: ConfigMapKeyRef is configmap selector for Semaphore configuration

fields.name: Namespace
fields.doc: Namespace is the namespace of the configmap, default: [namespace of workflow]

api_types.name: Mutex
api_types.doc: Mutex holds Mutex configuration

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: name of the mutex

fields.name: Namespace
fields.doc: Namespace is the namespace of the mutex, default: [namespace of workflow]

api_types.name: WorkflowTemplateRef
api_types.doc: WorkflowTemplateRef is a reference to a WorkflowTemplate resource.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the resource name of the workflow template.

fields.name: ClusterScope
fields.doc: ClusterScope indicates the referred template is cluster scoped (i.e. a ClusterWorkflowTemplate).

api_types.name: Arguments
api_types.doc: Arguments to a template

fields.name: Parameters
fields.doc: Parameters is the list of parameters to pass to the template or workflow +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: Artifacts
fields.doc: Artifacts is the list of artifacts to pass to the template or workflow +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

api_types.name: UserContainer
api_types.doc: UserContainer is a container specified by a user.

fields.name: MirrorVolumeMounts
fields.doc: MirrorVolumeMounts will mount the same volumes specified in the main container to the container (including artifacts), at the same mountPaths. This enables dind daemon to partially see the same filesystem as the main container in order to use features such as docker volume binding

api_types.name: WorkflowStatus
api_types.doc: WorkflowStatus contains overall status information about a workflow

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Phase a simple, high-level summary of where the workflow is in its lifecycle. Will be "" (Unknown), "Pending", or "Running" before the workflow is completed, and "Succeeded", "Failed" or "Error" once the workflow has completed.

fields.name: StartedAt
fields.doc: Time at which this workflow started

fields.name: FinishedAt
fields.doc: Time at which this workflow completed

fields.name: EstimatedDuration
fields.doc: EstimatedDuration in seconds.

fields.name: Progress
fields.doc: Progress to completion

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: A human readable message indicating details about why the workflow is in this condition.

fields.name: CompressedNodes
fields.doc: Compressed and base64 decoded Nodes map

fields.name: Nodes
fields.doc: Nodes is a mapping between a node ID and the node's status.

fields.name: OffloadNodeStatusVersion
fields.doc: Whether on not node status has been offloaded to a database. If exists, then Nodes and CompressedNodes will be empty. This will actually be populated with a hash of the offloaded data.

fields.name: StoredTemplates
fields.doc: StoredTemplates is a mapping between a template ref and the node's status.

fields.name: PersistentVolumeClaims
fields.doc: PersistentVolumeClaims tracks all PVCs that were created as part of the workflow. The contents of this list are drained at the end of the workflow.

fields.name: Outputs
fields.doc: Outputs captures output values and artifact locations produced by the workflow via global outputs

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Conditions is a list of conditions the Workflow may have

fields.name: ResourcesDuration
fields.doc: ResourcesDuration is the total for the workflow

fields.name: StoredWorkflowSpec
fields.doc: StoredWorkflowSpec stores the WorkflowTemplate spec for future execution.

fields.name: Synchronization
fields.doc: Synchronization stores the status of synchronization locks

fields.name: ArtifactRepositoryRef
fields.doc: ArtifactRepositoryRef is used to cache the repository to use so we do not need to determine it everytime we reconcile.

fields.name: ArtifactGCStatus
fields.doc: ArtifactGCStatus maintains the status of Artifact Garbage Collection

fields.name: TaskResultsCompletionStatus
fields.doc: TaskResultsCompletionStatus tracks task result completion status (mapped by node ID). Used to prevent premature archiving and garbage collection.

api_types.name: Backoff
api_types.doc: Backoff is a backoff strategy to use within retryStrategy

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration is the amount to back off. Default unit is seconds, but could also be a duration (e.g. "2m", "1h")

fields.name: Factor
fields.doc: Factor is a factor to multiply the base duration after each failed retry

fields.name: MaxDuration
fields.doc: MaxDuration is the maximum amount of time allowed for a workflow in the backoff strategy. It is important to note that if the workflow template includes activeDeadlineSeconds, the pod's deadline is initially set with activeDeadlineSeconds. However, when the workflow fails, the pod's deadline is then overridden by maxDuration. This ensures that the workflow does not exceed the specified maximum duration when retries are involved.

api_types.name: RetryAffinity
api_types.doc: RetryAffinity prevents running steps on the same host.

api_types.name: RetryStrategy
api_types.doc: RetryStrategy provides controls on how to retry a workflow step

fields.name: Limit
fields.doc: Limit is the maximum number of retry attempts when retrying a container. It does not include the original container; the maximum number of total attempts will be `limit + 1`.

fields.name: RetryPolicy
fields.doc: RetryPolicy is a policy of NodePhase statuses that will be retried

fields.name: Backoff
fields.doc: Backoff is a backoff strategy

fields.name: Affinity
fields.doc: Affinity prevents running workflow's step on the same host

fields.name: Expression
fields.doc: Expression is a condition expression for when a node will be retried. If it evaluates to false, the node will not be retried and the retry strategy will be ignored

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type is the type of condition

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: Status is the status of the condition

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Message is the condition message

api_types.name: NodeStatus
api_types.doc: NodeStatus contains status information about an individual node in the workflow

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: ID is a unique identifier of a node within the worklow It is implemented as a hash of the node name, which makes the ID deterministic

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is unique name in the node tree used to generate the node ID

fields.name: DisplayName
fields.doc: DisplayName is a human readable representation of the node. Unique within a template boundary

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type indicates type of node

fields.name: TemplateName
fields.doc: TemplateName is the template name which this node corresponds to. Not applicable to virtual nodes (e.g. Retry, StepGroup)

fields.name: TemplateRef
fields.doc: TemplateRef is the reference to the template resource which this node corresponds to. Not applicable to virtual nodes (e.g. Retry, StepGroup)

fields.name: TemplateScope
fields.doc: TemplateScope is the template scope in which the template of this node was retrieved.

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Phase a simple, high-level summary of where the node is in its lifecycle. Can be used as a state machine. Will be one of these values "Pending", "Running" before the node is completed, or "Succeeded", "Skipped", "Failed", "Error", or "Omitted" as a final state.

fields.name: BoundaryID
fields.doc: BoundaryID indicates the node ID of the associated template root node in which this node belongs to

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: A human readable message indicating details about why the node is in this condition.

fields.name: StartedAt
fields.doc: Time at which this node started

fields.name: FinishedAt
fields.doc: Time at which this node completed

fields.name: EstimatedDuration
fields.doc: EstimatedDuration in seconds.

fields.name: Progress
fields.doc: Progress to completion

fields.name: ResourcesDuration
fields.doc: ResourcesDuration is indicative, but not accurate, resource duration. This is populated when the nodes completes.

fields.name: PodIP
fields.doc: PodIP captures the IP of the pod for daemoned steps

fields.name: Daemoned
fields.doc: Daemoned tracks whether or not this node was daemoned and need to be terminated

fields.name: NodeFlag
fields.doc: NodeFlag tracks some history of node. e.g.) hooked, retried, etc.

fields.name: Inputs
fields.doc: Inputs captures input parameter values and artifact locations supplied to this template invocation

fields.name: Outputs
fields.doc: Outputs captures output parameter values and artifact locations produced by this template invocation

fields.name: Children
fields.doc: Children is a list of child node IDs

fields.name: OutboundNodes
fields.doc: OutboundNodes tracks the node IDs which are considered "outbound" nodes to a template invocation. For every invocation of a template, there are nodes which we considered as "outbound". Essentially, these are last nodes in the execution sequence to run, before the template is considered completed. These nodes are then connected as parents to a following step. In the case of single pod steps (i.e. container, script, resource templates), this list will be nil since the pod itself is already considered the "outbound" node. In the case of DAGs, outbound nodes are the "target" tasks (tasks with no children). In the case of steps, outbound nodes are all the containers involved in the last step group. NOTE: since templates are composable, the list of outbound nodes are carried upwards when a DAG/steps template invokes another DAG/steps template. In other words, the outbound nodes of a template, will be a superset of the outbound nodes of its last children.

fields.name: HostNodeName
fields.doc: HostNodeName name of the Kubernetes node on which the Pod is running, if applicable

fields.name: MemoizationStatus
fields.doc: MemoizationStatus holds information about cached nodes

fields.name: SynchronizationStatus
fields.doc: SynchronizationStatus is the synchronization status of the node

api_types.name: WorkflowList
api_types.doc: WorkflowList is list of Workflow resources +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object

api_types.name: S3Bucket
api_types.doc: S3Bucket contains the access information required for interfacing with an S3 bucket

fields.name: Endpoint
fields.doc: Endpoint is the hostname of the bucket endpoint

fields.name: Bucket
fields.doc: Bucket is the name of the bucket

fields.name: Region
fields.doc: Region contains the optional bucket region

fields.name: Insecure
fields.doc: Insecure will connect to the service with TLS

fields.name: AccessKeySecret
fields.doc: AccessKeySecret is the secret selector to the bucket's access key

fields.name: SecretKeySecret
fields.doc: SecretKeySecret is the secret selector to the bucket's secret key

fields.name: SessionTokenSecret
fields.doc: SessionTokenSecret is used for ephemeral credentials like an IAM assume role or S3 access grant

fields.name: RoleARN
fields.doc: RoleARN is the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role to assume.

fields.name: UseSDKCreds
fields.doc: UseSDKCreds tells the driver to figure out credentials based on sdk defaults.

fields.name: CreateBucketIfNotPresent
fields.doc: CreateBucketIfNotPresent tells the driver to attempt to create the S3 bucket for output artifacts, if it doesn't exist. Setting Enabled Encryption will apply either SSE-S3 to the bucket if KmsKeyId is not set or SSE-KMS if it is.

fields.name: CASecret
fields.doc: CASecret specifies the secret that contains the CA, used to verify the TLS connection

api_types.name: S3EncryptionOptions
api_types.doc: S3EncryptionOptions used to determine encryption options during s3 operations

fields.name: KmsKeyId
fields.doc: KMSKeyId tells the driver to encrypt the object using the specified KMS Key.

fields.name: KmsEncryptionContext
fields.doc: KmsEncryptionContext is a json blob that contains an encryption context. See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/concepts.html#encrypt_context for more information

fields.name: EnableEncryption
fields.doc: EnableEncryption tells the driver to encrypt objects if set to true. If kmsKeyId and serverSideCustomerKeySecret are not set, SSE-S3 will be used

fields.name: ServerSideCustomerKeySecret
fields.doc: ServerSideCustomerKeySecret tells the driver to encrypt the output artifacts using SSE-C with the specified secret.

api_types.name: CreateS3BucketOptions
api_types.doc: CreateS3BucketOptions options used to determine automatic automatic bucket-creation process

fields.name: ObjectLocking
fields.doc: ObjectLocking Enable object locking

api_types.name: S3Artifact
api_types.doc: S3Artifact is the location of an S3 artifact

fields.name: Key
fields.doc: Key is the key in the bucket where the artifact resides

api_types.name: GitArtifact
api_types.doc: GitArtifact is the location of an git artifact

fields.name: Repo
fields.doc: Repo is the git repository

fields.name: Revision
fields.doc: Revision is the git commit, tag, branch to checkout

fields.name: Depth
fields.doc: Depth specifies clones/fetches should be shallow and include the given number of commits from the branch tip

fields.name: Fetch
fields.doc: Fetch specifies a number of refs that should be fetched before checkout

fields.name: UsernameSecret
fields.doc: UsernameSecret is the secret selector to the repository username

fields.name: PasswordSecret
fields.doc: PasswordSecret is the secret selector to the repository password

fields.name: SSHPrivateKeySecret
fields.doc: SSHPrivateKeySecret is the secret selector to the repository ssh private key

fields.name: InsecureIgnoreHostKey
fields.doc: InsecureIgnoreHostKey disables SSH strict host key checking during git clone

fields.name: DisableSubmodules
fields.doc: DisableSubmodules disables submodules during git clone

fields.name: SingleBranch
fields.doc: SingleBranch enables single branch clone, using the `branch` parameter

fields.name: Branch
fields.doc: Branch is the branch to fetch when `SingleBranch` is enabled

fields.name: InsecureSkipTLS
fields.doc: InsecureSkipTLS disables server certificate verification resulting in insecure HTTPS connections

api_types.name: TTLStrategy
api_types.doc: TTLStrategy is the strategy for the time to live depending on if the workflow succeeded or failed

fields.name: SecondsAfterCompletion
fields.doc: SecondsAfterCompletion is the number of seconds to live after completion

fields.name: SecondsAfterSuccess
fields.doc: SecondsAfterSuccess is the number of seconds to live after success

fields.name: SecondsAfterFailure
fields.doc: SecondsAfterFailure is the number of seconds to live after failure

api_types.name: ArtifactoryAuth
api_types.doc: ArtifactoryAuth describes the secret selectors required for authenticating to artifactory

fields.name: UsernameSecret
fields.doc: UsernameSecret is the secret selector to the repository username

fields.name: PasswordSecret
fields.doc: PasswordSecret is the secret selector to the repository password

api_types.name: ArtifactoryArtifact
api_types.doc: ArtifactoryArtifact is the location of an artifactory artifact

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: URL of the artifact

api_types.name: AzureBlobContainer
api_types.doc: AzureBlobContainer contains the access information for interfacing with an Azure Blob Storage container

fields.name: Endpoint
fields.doc: Endpoint is the service url associated with an account. It is most likely "https://<ACCOUNT_NAME>.blob.core.windows.net"

fields.name: Container
fields.doc: Container is the container where resources will be stored

fields.name: AccountKeySecret
fields.doc: AccountKeySecret is the secret selector to the Azure Blob Storage account access key

fields.name: UseSDKCreds
fields.doc: UseSDKCreds tells the driver to figure out credentials based on sdk defaults.

api_types.name: WorkflowSpec
api_types.doc: WorkflowSpec is the specification of a Workflow.

fields.name: Templates
fields.doc: Templates is a list of workflow templates used in a workflow +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: Entrypoint
fields.doc: Entrypoint is a template reference to the starting point of the workflow.

fields.name: Arguments
fields.doc: Arguments contain the parameters and artifacts sent to the workflow entrypoint Parameters are referencable globally using the 'workflow' variable prefix. e.g. {{workflow.parameters.myparam}}

fields.name: ServiceAccountName
fields.doc: ServiceAccountName is the name of the ServiceAccount to run all pods of the workflow as.

fields.name: AutomountServiceAccountToken
fields.doc: AutomountServiceAccountToken indicates whether a service account token should be automatically mounted in pods. ServiceAccountName of ExecutorConfig must be specified if this value is false.

fields.name: Executor
fields.doc: Executor holds configurations of executor containers of the workflow.

fields.name: Volumes
fields.doc: Volumes is a list of volumes that can be mounted by containers in a workflow. +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: VolumeClaimTemplates
fields.doc: VolumeClaimTemplates is a list of claims that containers are allowed to reference. The Workflow controller will create the claims at the beginning of the workflow and delete the claims upon completion of the workflow

fields.name: Parallelism
fields.doc: Parallelism limits the max total parallel pods that can execute at the same time in a workflow

fields.name: ArtifactRepositoryRef
fields.doc: ArtifactRepositoryRef specifies the configMap name and key containing the artifact repository config.

fields.name: Suspend
fields.doc: Suspend will suspend the workflow and prevent execution of any future steps in the workflow

fields.name: NodeSelector
fields.doc: NodeSelector is a selector which will result in all pods of the workflow to be scheduled on the selected node(s). This is able to be overridden by a nodeSelector specified in the template.

fields.name: Affinity
fields.doc: Affinity sets the scheduling constraints for all pods in the workflow. Can be overridden by an affinity specified in the template

fields.name: Tolerations
fields.doc: Tolerations to apply to workflow pods. +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=key

fields.name: ImagePullSecrets
fields.doc: ImagePullSecrets is a list of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: HostNetwork
fields.doc: Host networking requested for this workflow pod. Default to false.

fields.name: DNSPolicy
fields.doc: Set DNS policy for workflow pods. Defaults to "ClusterFirst". Valid values are 'ClusterFirstWithHostNet', 'ClusterFirst', 'Default' or 'None'. DNS parameters given in DNSConfig will be merged with the policy selected with DNSPolicy. To have DNS options set along with hostNetwork, you have to specify DNS policy explicitly to 'ClusterFirstWithHostNet'.

fields.name: DNSConfig
fields.doc: PodDNSConfig defines the DNS parameters of a pod in addition to those generated from DNSPolicy.

fields.name: OnExit
fields.doc: OnExit is a template reference which is invoked at the end of the workflow, irrespective of the success, failure, or error of the primary workflow.

fields.name: TTLStrategy
fields.doc: TTLStrategy limits the lifetime of a Workflow that has finished execution depending on if it Succeeded or Failed. If this struct is set, once the Workflow finishes, it will be deleted after the time to live expires. If this field is unset, the controller config map will hold the default values.

fields.name: ActiveDeadlineSeconds
fields.doc: Optional duration in seconds relative to the workflow start time which the workflow is allowed to run before the controller terminates the workflow. A value of zero is used to terminate a Running workflow

fields.name: Priority
fields.doc: Priority is used if controller is configured to process limited number of workflows in parallel. Workflows with higher priority are processed first.

fields.name: SchedulerName
fields.doc: Set scheduler name for all pods. Will be overridden if container/script template's scheduler name is set. Default scheduler will be used if neither specified.

fields.name: PodGC
fields.doc: PodGC describes the strategy to use when deleting completed pods

fields.name: PodPriorityClassName
fields.doc: PriorityClassName to apply to workflow pods.

fields.name: PodPriority
fields.doc: Priority to apply to workflow pods. DEPRECATED: Use PodPriorityClassName instead.

fields.name: HostAliases
fields.doc: +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=ip

fields.name: SecurityContext
fields.doc: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings. Optional: Defaults to empty.  See type description for default values of each field.

fields.name: PodSpecPatch
fields.doc: PodSpecPatch holds strategic merge patch to apply against the pod spec. Allows parameterization of container fields which are not strings (e.g. resource limits).

fields.name: PodDisruptionBudget
fields.doc: PodDisruptionBudget holds the number of concurrent disruptions that you allow for Workflow's Pods. Controller will automatically add the selector with workflow name, if selector is empty. Optional: Defaults to empty.

fields.name: Metrics
fields.doc: Metrics are a list of metrics emitted from this Workflow

fields.name: Shutdown
fields.doc: Shutdown will shutdown the workflow according to its ShutdownStrategy

fields.name: WorkflowTemplateRef
fields.doc: WorkflowTemplateRef holds a reference to a WorkflowTemplate for execution

fields.name: Synchronization
fields.doc: Synchronization holds synchronization lock configuration for this Workflow

fields.name: VolumeClaimGC
fields.doc: VolumeClaimGC describes the strategy to use when deleting volumes from completed workflows

fields.name: RetryStrategy
fields.doc: RetryStrategy for all templates in the workflow.

fields.name: PodMetadata
fields.doc: PodMetadata defines additional metadata that should be applied to workflow pods

fields.name: TemplateDefaults
fields.doc: TemplateDefaults holds default template values that will apply to all templates in the Workflow, unless overridden on the template-level

fields.name: ArchiveLogs
fields.doc: ArchiveLogs indicates if the container logs should be archived

fields.name: Hooks
fields.doc: Hooks holds the lifecycle hook which is invoked at lifecycle of step, irrespective of the success, failure, or error status of the primary step

fields.name: WorkflowMetadata
fields.doc: WorkflowMetadata contains some metadata of the workflow to refer to

fields.name: ArtifactGC
fields.doc: ArtifactGC describes the strategy to use when deleting artifacts from completed or deleted workflows (applies to all output Artifacts unless Artifact.ArtifactGC is specified, which overrides this)

api_types.name: AzureArtifact
api_types.doc: AzureArtifact is the location of a an Azure Storage artifact

fields.name: Blob
fields.doc: Blob is the blob name (i.e., path) in the container where the artifact resides

api_types.name: HDFSArtifact
api_types.doc: HDFSArtifact is the location of an HDFS artifact

fields.name: Path
fields.doc: Path is a file path in HDFS

fields.name: Force
fields.doc: Force copies a file forcibly even if it exists

api_types.name: HDFSConfig
api_types.doc: HDFSConfig is configurations for HDFS

fields.name: Addresses
fields.doc: Addresses is accessible addresses of HDFS name nodes

fields.name: HDFSUser
fields.doc: HDFSUser is the user to access HDFS file system. It is ignored if either ccache or keytab is used.

fields.name: DataTransferProtection
fields.doc: DataTransferProtection is the protection level for HDFS data transfer. It corresponds to the dfs.data.transfer.protection configuration in HDFS.

api_types.name: HDFSKrbConfig
api_types.doc: HDFSKrbConfig is auth configurations for Kerberos

fields.name: KrbCCacheSecret
fields.doc: KrbCCacheSecret is the secret selector for Kerberos ccache Either ccache or keytab can be set to use Kerberos.

fields.name: KrbKeytabSecret
fields.doc: KrbKeytabSecret is the secret selector for Kerberos keytab Either ccache or keytab can be set to use Kerberos.

fields.name: KrbUsername
fields.doc: KrbUsername is the Kerberos username used with Kerberos keytab It must be set if keytab is used.

fields.name: KrbRealm
fields.doc: KrbRealm is the Kerberos realm used with Kerberos keytab It must be set if keytab is used.

fields.name: KrbConfigConfigMap
fields.doc: KrbConfig is the configmap selector for Kerberos config as string It must be set if either ccache or keytab is used.

fields.name: KrbServicePrincipalName
fields.doc: KrbServicePrincipalName is the principal name of Kerberos service It must be set if either ccache or keytab is used.

api_types.name: RawArtifact
api_types.doc: RawArtifact allows raw string content to be placed as an artifact in a container

fields.name: Data
fields.doc: Data is the string contents of the artifact

api_types.name: Header
api_types.doc: Header indicate a key-value request header to be used when fetching artifacts over HTTP

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the header name

fields.name: Value
fields.doc: Value is the literal value to use for the header

api_types.name: BasicAuth
api_types.doc: BasicAuth describes the secret selectors required for basic authentication

fields.name: UsernameSecret
fields.doc: UsernameSecret is the secret selector to the repository username

fields.name: PasswordSecret
fields.doc: PasswordSecret is the secret selector to the repository password

api_types.name: ClientCertAuth
api_types.doc: ClientCertAuth holds necessary information for client authentication via certificates

api_types.name: OAuth2Auth
api_types.doc: OAuth2Auth holds all information for client authentication via OAuth2 tokens

api_types.name: OAuth2EndpointParam
api_types.doc: EndpointParam is for requesting optional fields that should be sent in the oauth request

fields.name: Key
fields.doc: Name is the header name

fields.name: Value
fields.doc: Value is the literal value to use for the header

api_types.name: HTTPArtifact
api_types.doc: HTTPArtifact allows a file served on HTTP to be placed as an input artifact in a container

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: URL of the artifact

fields.name: Headers
fields.doc: Headers are an optional list of headers to send with HTTP requests for artifacts

fields.name: Auth
fields.doc: Auth contains information for client authentication

api_types.name: GCSBucket
api_types.doc: GCSBucket contains the access information for interfacring with a GCS bucket

fields.name: Bucket
fields.doc: Bucket is the name of the bucket

fields.name: ServiceAccountKeySecret
fields.doc: ServiceAccountKeySecret is the secret selector to the bucket's service account key

api_types.name: GCSArtifact
api_types.doc: GCSArtifact is the location of a GCS artifact

fields.name: Key
fields.doc: Key is the path in the bucket where the artifact resides

api_types.name: OSSBucket
api_types.doc: OSSBucket contains the access information required for interfacing with an Alibaba Cloud OSS bucket

fields.name: Endpoint
fields.doc: Endpoint is the hostname of the bucket endpoint

fields.name: Bucket
fields.doc: Bucket is the name of the bucket

fields.name: AccessKeySecret
fields.doc: AccessKeySecret is the secret selector to the bucket's access key

fields.name: SecretKeySecret
fields.doc: SecretKeySecret is the secret selector to the bucket's secret key

fields.name: CreateBucketIfNotPresent
fields.doc: CreateBucketIfNotPresent tells the driver to attempt to create the OSS bucket for output artifacts, if it doesn't exist

fields.name: SecurityToken
fields.doc: SecurityToken is the user's temporary security token. For more details, check out: https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/100624.htm

fields.name: LifecycleRule
fields.doc: LifecycleRule specifies how to manage bucket's lifecycle

fields.name: UseSDKCreds
fields.doc: UseSDKCreds tells the driver to figure out credentials based on sdk defaults.

api_types.name: OSSArtifact
api_types.doc: OSSArtifact is the location of an Alibaba Cloud OSS artifact

fields.name: Key
fields.doc: Key is the path in the bucket where the artifact resides

api_types.name: OSSLifecycleRule
api_types.doc: OSSLifecycleRule specifies how to manage bucket's lifecycle

fields.name: MarkInfrequentAccessAfterDays
fields.doc: MarkInfrequentAccessAfterDays is the number of days before we convert the objects in the bucket to Infrequent Access (IA) storage type

fields.name: MarkDeletionAfterDays
fields.doc: MarkDeletionAfterDays is the number of days before we delete objects in the bucket

api_types.name: ExecutorConfig
api_types.doc: ExecutorConfig holds configurations of an executor container.

fields.name: ServiceAccountName
fields.doc: ServiceAccountName specifies the service account name of the executor container.

api_types.name: ScriptTemplate
api_types.doc: ScriptTemplate is a template subtype to enable scripting through code steps

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Source contains the source code of the script to execute

api_types.name: ResourceTemplate
api_types.doc: ResourceTemplate is a template subtype to manipulate kubernetes resources

fields.name: Action
fields.doc: Action is the action to perform to the resource. Must be one of: get, create, apply, delete, replace, patch

fields.name: MergeStrategy
fields.doc: MergeStrategy is the strategy used to merge a patch. It defaults to "strategic" Must be one of: strategic, merge, json

fields.name: Manifest
fields.doc: Manifest contains the kubernetes manifest

fields.name: ManifestFrom
fields.doc: ManifestFrom is the source for a single kubernetes manifest

fields.name: SetOwnerReference
fields.doc: SetOwnerReference sets the reference to the workflow on the OwnerReference of generated resource.

fields.name: SuccessCondition
fields.doc: SuccessCondition is a label selector expression which describes the conditions of the k8s resource in which it is acceptable to proceed to the following step

fields.name: FailureCondition
fields.doc: FailureCondition is a label selector expression which describes the conditions of the k8s resource in which the step was considered failed

fields.name: Flags
fields.doc: Flags is a set of additional options passed to kubectl before submitting a resource I.e. to disable resource validation: flags: [ "--validate=false"  # disable resource validation ]

fields.name: Artifact
fields.doc: Artifact contains the artifact to use

api_types.name: DAGTemplate
api_types.doc: DAGTemplate is a template subtype for directed acyclic graph templates

fields.name: Target
fields.doc: Target are one or more names of targets to execute in a DAG

fields.name: Tasks
fields.doc: Tasks are a list of DAG tasks +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: FailFast
fields.doc: This flag is for DAG logic. The DAG logic has a built-in "fail fast" feature to stop scheduling new steps, as soon as it detects that one of the DAG nodes is failed. Then it waits until all DAG nodes are completed before failing the DAG itself. The FailFast flag default is true,  if set to false, it will allow a DAG to run all branches of the DAG to completion (either success or failure), regardless of the failed outcomes of branches in the DAG. More info and example about this feature at https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/issues/1442

api_types.name: DAGTask
api_types.doc: DAGTask represents a node in the graph during DAG execution

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the name of the target

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Name of template to execute

fields.name: Inline
fields.doc: Inline is the template. Template must be empty if this is declared (and vice-versa).

fields.name: Arguments
fields.doc: Arguments are the parameter and artifact arguments to the template

fields.name: TemplateRef
fields.doc: TemplateRef is the reference to the template resource to execute.

fields.name: Dependencies
fields.doc: Dependencies are name of other targets which this depends on

fields.name: WithItems
fields.doc: WithItems expands a task into multiple parallel tasks from the items in the list

fields.name: WithParam
fields.doc: WithParam expands a task into multiple parallel tasks from the value in the parameter, which is expected to be a JSON list.

fields.name: WithSequence
fields.doc: WithSequence expands a task into a numeric sequence

fields.name: When
fields.doc: When is an expression in which the task should conditionally execute

fields.name: ContinueOn
fields.doc: ContinueOn makes argo to proceed with the following step even if this step fails. Errors and Failed states can be specified

fields.name: OnExit
fields.doc: OnExit is a template reference which is invoked at the end of the template, irrespective of the success, failure, or error of the primary template. DEPRECATED: Use Hooks[exit].Template instead.

fields.name: Depends
fields.doc: Depends are name of other targets which this depends on

fields.name: Hooks
fields.doc: Hooks hold the lifecycle hook which is invoked at lifecycle of task, irrespective of the success, failure, or error status of the primary task

api_types.name: SuspendTemplate
api_types.doc: SuspendTemplate is a template subtype to suspend a workflow at a predetermined point in time

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration is the seconds to wait before automatically resuming a template. Must be a string. Default unit is seconds. Could also be a Duration, e.g.: "2m", "6h"

api_types.name: ContinueOn
api_types.doc: ContinueOn defines if a workflow should continue even if a task or step fails/errors. It can be specified if the workflow should continue when the pod errors, fails or both.

api_types.name: Metrics
api_types.doc: Metrics are a list of metrics emitted from a Workflow/Template

fields.name: Prometheus
fields.doc: Prometheus is a list of prometheus metrics to be emitted

api_types.name: Prometheus
api_types.doc: Prometheus is a prometheus metric to be emitted

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the name of the metric

fields.name: Labels
fields.doc: Labels is a list of metric labels

fields.name: Help
fields.doc: Help is a string that describes the metric

fields.name: When
fields.doc: When is a conditional statement that decides when to emit the metric

fields.name: Gauge
fields.doc: Gauge is a gauge metric

fields.name: Histogram
fields.doc: Histogram is a histogram metric

fields.name: Counter
fields.doc: Counter is a counter metric

api_types.name: MetricLabel
api_types.doc: MetricLabel is a single label for a prometheus metric

api_types.name: Gauge
api_types.doc: Gauge is a Gauge prometheus metric

fields.name: Value
fields.doc: Value is the value to be used in the operation with the metric's current value. If no operation is set, value is the value of the metric

fields.name: Realtime
fields.doc: Realtime emits this metric in real time if applicable

fields.name: Operation
fields.doc: Operation defines the operation to apply with value and the metrics' current value

api_types.name: Histogram
api_types.doc: Histogram is a Histogram prometheus metric

fields.name: Value
fields.doc: Value is the value of the metric

fields.name: Buckets
fields.doc: Buckets is a list of bucket divisors for the histogram

api_types.name: Counter
api_types.doc: Counter is a Counter prometheus metric

fields.name: Value
fields.doc: Value is the value of the metric

api_types.name: Memoize
api_types.doc: Memoization enables caching for the Outputs of the template

fields.name: Key
fields.doc: Key is the key to use as the caching key

fields.name: Cache
fields.doc: Cache sets and configures the kind of cache

fields.name: MaxAge
fields.doc: MaxAge is the maximum age (e.g. "180s", "24h") of an entry that is still considered valid. If an entry is older than the MaxAge, it will be ignored.

api_types.name: MemoizationStatus
api_types.doc: MemoizationStatus is the status of this memoized node

fields.name: Hit
fields.doc: Hit indicates whether this node was created from a cache entry

fields.name: Key
fields.doc: Key is the name of the key used for this node's cache

fields.name: CacheName
fields.doc: Cache is the name of the cache that was used

api_types.name: Cache
api_types.doc: Cache is the configuration for the type of cache to be used

fields.name: ConfigMap
fields.doc: ConfigMap sets a ConfigMap-based cache

fields.name: Semaphore
fields.doc: Semaphore stores the semaphore name.

fields.name: Holders
fields.doc: Holders stores the list of current holder names in the workflow. +listType=atomic

fields.name: Holding
fields.doc: Holding stores the list of resource acquired synchronization lock for workflows.

fields.name: Waiting
fields.doc: Waiting indicates the list of current synchronization lock holders.

api_types.name: MutexHolding
api_types.doc: MutexHolding describes the mutex and the object which is holding it.

fields.name: Mutex
fields.doc: Reference for the mutex e.g: ${namespace}/mutex/${mutexName}

fields.name: Holder
fields.doc: Holder is a reference to the object which holds the Mutex. Holding Scenario: 1. Current workflow's NodeID which is holding the lock. e.g: ${NodeID} Waiting Scenario: 1. Current workflow or other workflow NodeID which is holding the lock. e.g: ${WorkflowName}/${NodeID}

api_types.name: MutexStatus
api_types.doc: MutexStatus contains which objects hold  mutex locks, and which objects this workflow is waiting on to release locks.

fields.name: Holding
fields.doc: Holding is a list of mutexes and their respective objects that are held by mutex lock for this workflow. +listType=atomic

fields.name: Waiting
fields.doc: Waiting is a list of mutexes and their respective objects this workflow is waiting for. +listType=atomic

api_types.name: SynchronizationStatus
api_types.doc: SynchronizationStatus stores the status of semaphore and mutex.

fields.name: Semaphore
fields.doc: Semaphore stores this workflow's Semaphore holder details

fields.name: Mutex
fields.doc: Mutex stores this workflow's mutex holder details

api_types.name: NodeSynchronizationStatus
api_types.doc: NodeSynchronizationStatus stores the status of a node

fields.name: Waiting
fields.doc: Waiting is the name of the lock that this node is waiting for

fields.name: Hooked
fields.doc: Hooked tracks whether or not this node was triggered by hook or onExit

fields.name: Retried
fields.doc: Retried tracks whether or not this node was retried by retryStrategy

api_types.name: Template
api_types.doc: Template is a reusable and composable unit of execution in a workflow

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the name of the template

fields.name: Inputs
fields.doc: Inputs describe what inputs parameters and artifacts are supplied to this template

fields.name: Outputs
fields.doc: Outputs describe the parameters and artifacts that this template produces

fields.name: NodeSelector
fields.doc: NodeSelector is a selector to schedule this step of the workflow to be run on the selected node(s). Overrides the selector set at the workflow level.

fields.name: Affinity
fields.doc: Affinity sets the pod's scheduling constraints Overrides the affinity set at the workflow level (if any)

fields.name: Metadata
fields.doc: Metdata sets the pods's metadata, i.e. annotations and labels

fields.name: Daemon
fields.doc: Daemon will allow a workflow to proceed to the next step so long as the container reaches readiness

fields.name: Steps
fields.doc: Steps define a series of sequential/parallel workflow steps

fields.name: Container
fields.doc: Container is the main container image to run in the pod

fields.name: ContainerSet
fields.doc: ContainerSet groups multiple containers within a single pod.

fields.name: Script
fields.doc: Script runs a portion of code against an interpreter

fields.name: Resource
fields.doc: Resource template subtype which can run k8s resources

fields.name: DAG
fields.doc: DAG template subtype which runs a DAG

fields.name: Suspend
fields.doc: Suspend template subtype which can suspend a workflow when reaching the step

fields.name: Data
fields.doc: Data is a data template

fields.name: HTTP
fields.doc: HTTP makes a HTTP request

fields.name: Plugin
fields.doc: Plugin is a plugin template

fields.name: Volumes
fields.doc: Volumes is a list of volumes that can be mounted by containers in a template. +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: InitContainers
fields.doc: InitContainers is a list of containers which run before the main container. +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: Sidecars
fields.doc: Sidecars is a list of containers which run alongside the main container Sidecars are automatically killed when the main container completes +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: ArchiveLocation
fields.doc: Location in which all files related to the step will be stored (logs, artifacts, etc...). Can be overridden by individual items in Outputs. If omitted, will use the default artifact repository location configured in the controller, appended with the <workflowname>/<nodename> in the key.

fields.name: ActiveDeadlineSeconds
fields.doc: Optional duration in seconds relative to the StartTime that the pod may be active on a node before the system actively tries to terminate the pod; value must be positive integer This field is only applicable to container and script templates.

fields.name: RetryStrategy
fields.doc: RetryStrategy describes how to retry a template when it fails

fields.name: Parallelism
fields.doc: Parallelism limits the max total parallel pods that can execute at the same time within the boundaries of this template invocation. If additional steps/dag templates are invoked, the pods created by those templates will not be counted towards this total.

fields.name: FailFast
fields.doc: FailFast, if specified, will fail this template if any of its child pods has failed. This is useful for when this template is expanded with `withItems`, etc.

fields.name: Tolerations
fields.doc: Tolerations to apply to workflow pods. +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=key

fields.name: SchedulerName
fields.doc: If specified, the pod will be dispatched by specified scheduler. Or it will be dispatched by workflow scope scheduler if specified. If neither specified, the pod will be dispatched by default scheduler.

fields.name: PriorityClassName
fields.doc: PriorityClassName to apply to workflow pods.

fields.name: Priority
fields.doc: Priority to apply to workflow pods.

fields.name: ServiceAccountName
fields.doc: ServiceAccountName to apply to workflow pods

fields.name: AutomountServiceAccountToken
fields.doc: AutomountServiceAccountToken indicates whether a service account token should be automatically mounted in pods. ServiceAccountName of ExecutorConfig must be specified if this value is false.

fields.name: Executor
fields.doc: Executor holds configurations of the executor container.

fields.name: HostAliases
fields.doc: HostAliases is an optional list of hosts and IPs that will be injected into the pod spec +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=ip

fields.name: SecurityContext
fields.doc: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings. Optional: Defaults to empty.  See type description for default values of each field.

fields.name: PodSpecPatch
fields.doc: PodSpecPatch holds strategic merge patch to apply against the pod spec. Allows parameterization of container fields which are not strings (e.g. resource limits).

fields.name: Metrics
fields.doc: Metrics are a list of metrics emitted from this template

fields.name: Synchronization
fields.doc: Synchronization holds synchronization lock configuration for this template

fields.name: Memoize
fields.doc: Memoize allows templates to use outputs generated from already executed templates

fields.name: Timeout
fields.doc: Timeout allows to set the total node execution timeout duration counting from the node's start time. This duration also includes time in which the node spends in Pending state. This duration may not be applied to Step or DAG templates.

api_types.name: Inputs
api_types.doc: Inputs are the mechanism for passing parameters, artifacts, volumes from one template to another

fields.name: Parameters
fields.doc: Parameters are a list of parameters passed as inputs +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

fields.name: Artifacts
fields.doc: Artifact are a list of artifacts passed as inputs +patchStrategy=merge +patchMergeKey=name

api_types.name: Metadata
api_types.doc: Pod metdata

api_types.name: Parameter
api_types.doc: Parameter indicate a passed string parameter to a service template with an optional default value

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the parameter name

fields.name: Default
fields.doc: Default is the default value to use for an input parameter if a value was not supplied

fields.name: Value
fields.doc: Value is the literal value to use for the parameter. If specified in the context of an input parameter, the value takes precedence over any passed values

fields.name: ValueFrom
fields.doc: ValueFrom is the source for the output parameter's value

fields.name: GlobalName
fields.doc: GlobalName exports an output parameter to the global scope, making it available as '{{workflow.outputs.parameters.XXXX}} and in workflow.status.outputs.parameters

fields.name: Enum
fields.doc: Enum holds a list of string values to choose from, for the actual value of the parameter

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Description is the parameter description

api_types.name: ValueFrom
api_types.doc: ValueFrom describes a location in which to obtain the value to a parameter

fields.name: Path
fields.doc: Path in the container to retrieve an output parameter value from in container templates

fields.name: JSONPath
fields.doc: JSONPath of a resource to retrieve an output parameter value from in resource templates

fields.name: JQFilter
fields.doc: JQFilter expression against the resource object in resource templates

fields.name: Event
fields.doc: Selector (https://github.com/expr-lang/expr) that is evaluated against the event to get the value of the parameter. E.g. `payload.message`

fields.name: Parameter
fields.doc: Parameter reference to a step or dag task in which to retrieve an output parameter value from (e.g. '{{steps.mystep.outputs.myparam}}')

fields.name: Supplied
fields.doc: Supplied value to be filled in directly, either through the CLI, API, etc.

fields.name: ConfigMapKeyRef
fields.doc: ConfigMapKeyRef is configmap selector for input parameter configuration

fields.name: Default
fields.doc: Default specifies a value to be used if retrieving the value from the specified source fails

fields.name: Expression
fields.doc: Expression, if defined, is evaluated to specify the value for the parameter

api_types.name: Artifact
api_types.doc: Artifact indicates an artifact to place at a specified path

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: name of the artifact. must be unique within a template's inputs/outputs.

fields.name: Path
fields.doc: Path is the container path to the artifact

fields.name: Mode
fields.doc: mode bits to use on this file, must be a value between 0 and 0777 set when loading input artifacts.

fields.name: From
fields.doc: From allows an artifact to reference an artifact from a previous step

fields.name: ArtifactLocation
fields.doc: ArtifactLocation contains the location of the artifact

fields.name: GlobalName
fields.doc: GlobalName exports an output artifact to the global scope, making it available as '{{workflow.outputs.artifacts.XXXX}} and in workflow.status.outputs.artifacts

fields.name: Archive
fields.doc: Archive controls how the artifact will be saved to the artifact repository.

fields.name: Optional
fields.doc: Make Artifacts optional, if Artifacts doesn't generate or exist

fields.name: SubPath
fields.doc: SubPath allows an artifact to be sourced from a subpath within the specified source

fields.name: RecurseMode
fields.doc: If mode is set, apply the permission recursively into the artifact if it is a folder

fields.name: FromExpression
fields.doc: FromExpression, if defined, is evaluated to specify the value for the artifact

fields.name: ArtifactGC
fields.doc: ArtifactGC describes the strategy to use when to deleting an artifact from completed or deleted workflows

fields.name: Deleted
fields.doc: Has this been deleted?

summary: argo-workflows is a Kubernetes component. connecting to external services: azure-blob, gcs, grpc. defining 6 API spec type(s) across 139 struct(s) with 562 total fields. with 6 operator config constants. reporting 7 status condition types.
